back_translation: I wish you would endure my foolishness a little — but you are already enduring me.
For I am protectively-zealous over you with the protective-zeal of God; I have betrothed you to one husband, in order to present you as a pure virgin to Christ.
But I fear that, just as the serpent deceived Eve by its cunning, your minds may be made corrupt away from the sincerity-of-heart and purity that is toward Christ.
For if a person comes and proclaims another Jesus whom we did not proclaim, or if you receive another spirit which you did not receive, or another gospel which you did not accept, you endure it well.
For I consider that I am not inferior to those 'top-tier apostles' even in the slightest.
Even though I am not skilled in speaking, I am not lacking in knowledge, and we have demonstrated this clearly to you in every matter and every way.
Have I sinned, in humbling myself so that you might be lifted up, because I proclaimed the gospel of God to you free of charge?
I robbed other churches by accepting wages from them in order to serve you.

And when I was with you and in need, I was not a burden to anyone, for the brothers who came from Macedonia filled up my lack. I have kept myself from being a burden to you in every matter, and I will keep doing so.
The truth of Christ is in me, that this pride of mine will not be silenced in the province of Achaia.
Why? Because I do not love you? God knows.
Whatever I do, I will continue to do, in order to cut off the opportunity of those who want an opportunity to boast, so that in the matter they boast about they will be found just like us.
For such people are false apostles, deceitful workers, who disguise themselves as apostles of Christ.
And it is not surprising, because Satan himself disguises himself as an angel of light.
Therefore, it is not a great thing if his servants disguise themselves as servants of righteousness. Their final-destiny will be according to their actions.
I say again, let no one consider me to be a fool, but if anyone does, then receive me as a fool, so that I too may take pride a little.
What I say, I do not say according to the manner of the Lord, but as if speaking in foolishness, in this confidence to take pride.
Since many take pride according to the flesh, I too will take pride.
For you who are wise endure fools with gladness.
For you endure even when someone makes you slaves, devours you, takes advantage of you, exalts himself, or strikes your face.
I speak to my dishonor that in this matter we were too weak. But in whatever anyone dares to take pride, I speak in foolishness — I too dare.
Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Israelites? So am I. Are they descendants of Abraham? So am I.
Are they servants of Christ? — I speak as a madman — I am even more, in laboring more, in being imprisoned more, in beatings beyond measure, and facing death many times.
I received the forty-stripes-minus-one from the Jews five times.
I was beaten with rods three times, stoned once, shipwrecked three times, and was in the deep sea for one day and one night.

In journeys often — dangers from rivers, dangers from robbers, dangers from my own people, dangers from Gentiles, dangers in the city, dangers in the wilderness, dangers in the sea, dangers among false brothers.
In labor and exhaustion, in lack of sleep often, in hunger and thirst, in fasting often, in cold and lack of clothing.
Apart from these external matters, there is the daily pressure on me, namely the concern about all the churches.
Who is weak, am I not weak too? Who is led to stumble in sin, do I not burn within?
If it is necessary to take pride, I will take pride in the things that show my weakness.
God and the Father of the Lord Jesus, the One who is blessed forever, knows that I am not lying.
In the city of Damascus, the governor of King Aretas guarded the city of the Damascenes in order to arrest me.
And I was lowered through a window in a basket through the wall, and escaped from his hands.
